The 8 routed experts are a FIXED LIST and the routing weights are a FIXED VECTOR; nothing here made a routing decision and no result may be read as though it had.

GLM52_KERNEL_SELECTION_MATRIX.json routed_expert 6144x2048 -> decode_fma_2d blk8 cbs32 tpg256 (decided by gpu median; that matrix ALSO reports this geometry as its one unstable shape, so the lookup-linear alternative is measured here, not assumed away)

byte_ledger.model: ANALYTIC graph ledger, not a counter reading

result.routing_weight_note: FIXED VECTOR, softmax of a seeded pseudo-logit; the shared expert's trailing 1.0 is not part of the softmax.  No router tensor exists to produce these.

wave_a.status: DERIVED (difference of two measured cumulative medians)

swiglu.status: DERIVED (difference of two measured cumulative medians)

wave_b.status: DERIVED (difference of two measured cumulative medians)

combine.status: DERIVED (difference of two measured cumulative medians)

_measurement_context.note: cumulative medians here come from the ROUND-ROBIN stage series, where each rep submits all four truncations; they run a little slower than the standalone full-graph measurement in `latency` and must not be compared to it.  Only the differences between them are used.

residual.status: FUSED into moe_combine -- the residual is the combine kernel's accumulator initialiser, so it has no separable cost

config.source: GLM52_KERNEL_SELECTION_MATRIX.json routed_expert 2048x6144 -> lookup_linear cbs16 tpg1024 blk48 (decided by executed_bytes)
GLM52_KERNEL_SELECTION_MATRIX.json shared_expert 6144x2048 -> lookup_linear cbs16 tpg1024 blk16

byte_ledger.model: ANALYTIC graph ledger, not a counter reading

host_encode_lever.note: a HOST lever: it moves wall and must not move the GPU clock.  If the GPU ratio is not ~1.0 the two arms are not the same graph.

swiglu_vec4_lever.note: reduce+SwiGLU walks `blocks` partials with a rows-wide stride; four rows per thread gives four independent accumulator chains and 16-byte loads for identical bytes.  Both arms are parity-graded; a verdict of NEUTRAL_WITHIN_NOISE means the vectorisation bought nothing and is kept only because it is already compiled.

parity_authority.implementation: gravity_forge.pq_execute per projection, composed through silu(gate)*up -> down -> routing weight -> shared -> residual
parity_authority.why_whole_layer: SwiGLU is nonlinear, so per-projection parity does not bound the layer's error; this is the composed figure

wall_median.note: the tightest target whose bound the MEASURED median clears; nothing rounded

wall_min.note: the tightest target whose bound the MEASURED median clears; nothing rounded

attention_per_layer_ms.source: sum of 5 selection-matrix per-tensor GPU medians (0.2026 ms) + one command-buffer fixed cost (0.2158 ms); no attention graph has been executed

dense_mlp_layer_ms.source: selection-matrix GPU medians for [6144,12288] x2 + [12288,6144] (0.1465 ms) + one command-buffer fixed cost

unmeasured_terms: embedding row gather -- on no safe shard
attention softmax, RoPE, KV cache reads and the KV cache itself
the router matvec itself -- the tensor is absent from the artifacts
per_token.note: a lower bound on token latency and therefore an UPPER bound on tok/s: every unmeasured term above adds time and none subtracts it

moe_layer_ms.source: whole-layer GPU median, this run -- excludes the 215.8 us per-command-buffer host cost

attention_per_layer_ms.source: sum of 5 selection-matrix per-tensor GPU medians (0.2026 ms) + one command-buffer fixed cost (0.2158 ms); no attention graph has been executed

dense_mlp_layer_ms.source: selection-matrix GPU medians for [6144,12288] x2 + [12288,6144] (0.1465 ms) + one command-buffer fixed cost

unmeasured_terms: embedding row gather -- on no safe shard
attention softmax, RoPE, KV cache reads and the KV cache itself
the router matvec itself -- the tensor is absent from the artifacts
per_token_gpu_clock.note: a lower bound on token latency and therefore an UPPER bound on tok/s: every unmeasured term above adds time and none subtracts it
